1.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ubayy ibn Malik (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Whoever lives to see his parents, or one of them, and then enters the Fire after that, may Allah keep him far away and cast him away.'
1.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ubayy ibn Malik (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Barangsiapa mendapati kedua orang tuanya atau salah seorang dari keduanya kemudian masuk neraka setelah itu, maka Allah menjauhkan dan menghinakannya.'

2.iwh_en: It was narrated from the uncle of 'Abd al-Rahman Abu al-Minhal ibn Salama al-Khuza'i that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said to the tribe of Aslam: 'Fast today.' They said: 'We have already eaten.' He said: 'Then fast the remainder of your day' — meaning the day of 'Ashura'.
2.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari paman 'Abd al-Rahman Abu al-Minhal ibn Salama al-Khuza'i bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda kepada Bani Aslam: 'Berpuasalah hari ini.' Mereka berkata: 'Kami sudah makan.' Beliau bersabda: 'Berpuasalah untuk sisa harimu ini' — yakni hari Asyura.'

3.iwh_en: It was narrated from a man from his people called Malik or Ibn Malik that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Any Muslim who takes in an orphan between two Muslim parents and includes him in his food and drink until he becomes self-sufficient, Paradise is assured for him without question. And any Muslim who frees a Muslim slave, that will be his redemption from the Fire. And whoever lives to see his parents, or one of them, and then enters the Fire, may Allah keep him far away.'
3.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari seorang lelaki dari kaumnya yang bernama Malik atau Ibn Malik bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Muslim mana pun yang menanggung anak yatim antara dua orang tua Muslim dalam makanan dan minumannya hingga anak itu mandiri, maka surga pasti baginya. Dan Muslim mana pun yang memerdekakan seorang hamba Muslim, itu menjadi tebusannya dari neraka. Dan barangsiapa mendapati kedua orang tuanya atau salah satunya lalu masuk neraka, maka Allah menjauhkannya.'

4.iwh_en: It was narrated from Malik ibn al-Harith, a man from among them, that he heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ say: 'Whoever takes in an orphan between two Muslim parents and includes him in his food and drink until he becomes self-sufficient, Paradise is assured for him without question. And whoever frees a Muslim man, that will be his redemption from the Fire — each limb redeemed for a limb.'
4.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Malik ibn al-Harith, seorang dari kalangan mereka, bahwa ia mendengar Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Barangsiapa menanggung anak yatim antara dua orang tua Muslim dalam makanan dan minumannya hingga ia mandiri, maka surga pasti baginya. Dan barangsiapa memerdekakan seorang Muslim, itu menjadi tebusannya dari neraka — setiap anggota badan dibalas dengan anggota badan.'

5.iwh_en: It was narrated from 'Amr ibn Salima, from his father, that they came as a delegation to the Prophet ﷺ and when they wished to leave, they said: 'O Messenger of Allah, who should lead us in prayer?' He said: 'Whoever among you has memorised the most Quran or taken the most of the Quran.' He said: There was no one among the people who had memorised from the Quran what I had memorised, though I was young, so they sent me forward to lead them, and I led them in prayer wearing a short garment.
5.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari 'Amr ibn Salima, dari ayahnya, bahwa mereka datang sebagai utusan kepada Nabi ﷺ dan ketika hendak pulang, mereka berkata: 'Ya Rasulullah, siapa yang akan mengimami kami?' Beliau bersabda: 'Yang paling banyak menghafal Al-Qur'an di antara kalian.' Ia berkata: Tidak ada seorang pun di antara kaum yang hafal Al-Qur'an sebanyak yang aku hafal, padahal aku masih kecil, sehingga mereka mendorongku untuk mengimami mereka.'

6.iwh_en: It was narrated from 'Amr ibn Salima, from his father, that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Put forward the one among you who knows the most Quran.' He said: They looked around and I was on a large encampment, so they brought me forward — I was a young boy — and I led them in prayer wearing a garment of mine. When I prostrated, the garment gathered up and a woman from among them said: 'Cover your posterior from us.'
6.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari 'Amr ibn Salima dari ayahnya bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Majukanlah yang paling banyak hafal Al-Qur'an di antara kalian.' Ia berkata: Mereka melihat-lihat dan aku berada di sebuah kemah besar, maka mereka memajukanku — aku masih bocah — dan aku mengimami mereka shalat dengan pakaianku. Ketika aku sujud, pakaian itu naik dan seorang wanita berkata: 'Tutupilah auratmu dari kami.'

7.iwh_en: It was narrated that they used to go and ask those who came from the expeditions, and they would narrate that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Put forward the one among you who knows the most Quran to lead you in prayer.' So they always put forward whoever had memorised the most.
7.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan bahwa mereka biasa mendatangi orang-orang yang datang dari ekspedisi dan bertanya, dan mereka menceritakan bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Majukanlah yang paling banyak hafal Al-Qur'an di antara kalian untuk mengimami shalat.' Maka mereka selalu memajukan siapa yang paling banyak hafalannya.'

8.iwh_en: It was narrated from al-'Adda' ibn Khalid ibn Hawdhah that he said: 'I saw the Messenger of Allah ﷺ giving a sermon while wearing two green garments.'
8.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari al-'Adda' ibn Khalid ibn Hawdhah bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku melihat Rasulullah ﷺ berkhutbah mengenakan dua pakaian berwarna hijau.'

9.iwh_en: It was narrated from al-'Adda' ibn Khalid ibn Hawdhah that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said regarding a tribulation or mentioned certain people: 'If you sit still, you will be successful and rightly guided. If you sit still, you will be successful and rightly guided.'
9.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari al-'Adda' ibn Khalid ibn Hawdhah bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda tentang suatu fitnah: 'Jika kalian diam dan tidak bergerak, kalian akan beruntung dan mendapat petunjuk. Jika kalian diam dan tidak bergerak, kalian akan beruntung dan mendapat petunjuk.'

10.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ahmad ibn Khunbash that he saw the Messenger of Allah ﷺ keep his arms away from his sides when prostrating.
10.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ahmad ibn Khunbash bahwa ia melihat Rasulullah ﷺ merenggangkan kedua lengannya dari sisi tubuhnya ketika sujud.'

11.iwh_en: It was narrated from his father that he said: 'I said, O Messenger of Allah, I am a man who is frequently ill. Give me permission to ferment drink in a leather vessel.' He said: 'I gave him permission, and when [the drink] became strong, he poured water over it.'
11.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari ayahnya bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku berkata: Ya Rasulullah, aku adalah orang yang sering sakit. Izinkanlah aku membuat nabidh dalam sebuah wadah kulit.' Beliau mengizinkannya, dan ketika sudah memabukkan, ia menuangkan air ke atasnya.'

12.iwh_en: It was narrated from a companion that he heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ say: 'The Hour will not come until certain tribes are swallowed up by the earth, until they are swallowed, until they are swallowed.'
12.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari seorang sahabat bahwa ia mendengar Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Kiamat tidak akan terjadi hingga beberapa suku ditelan bumi, hingga mereka ditelan, hingga mereka ditelan.'

13.iwh_en: It was narrated from Rafi' ibn 'Amr al-Muzani that he said: 'I heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ say: The Arabs will continue to have good in them as long as this practice of theirs lasts. When they abandon it, they will perish.'
13.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Rafi' ibn 'Amr al-Muzani bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku mendengar Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: Orang-orang Arab akan terus memiliki kebaikan selama mereka mempertahankan tradisi ini. Jika mereka meninggalkannya, mereka akan binasa.'

14.iwh_en: It was narrated from Rafi' ibn 'Amr al-Muzani that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ mentioned the Khawarij and said: 'They will shoot out of the religion as an arrow shoots out of its target, and they will not return to it. They are the worst of all creation.'
14.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Rafi' ibn 'Amr al-Muzani bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ menyebut Khawarij dan bersabda: 'Mereka keluar dari agama sebagaimana anak panah keluar dari sasarannya, dan mereka tidak akan kembali ke dalamnya. Mereka adalah seburuk-buruk makhluk.'

15.iwh_en: It was narrated from a young companion that he was brought to the Prophet ﷺ and the Prophet ﷺ said: 'O boy, why do you throw stones at the date palms?' He said: 'I replied: To eat.' He said: 'Do not throw stones at them, but eat what falls from them. May Allah fill your belly and beautify your face.'
15.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari seorang sahabat muda bahwa ia dibawa kepada Nabi ﷺ dan Nabi bersabda: 'Wahai anak kecil, mengapa engkau melempar pohon kurma?' Ia berkata: 'Aku menjawab: Untuk dimakan.' Beliau bersabda: 'Jangan melemparnya, tapi makanlah yang jatuh. Semoga Allah mengenyangkan perutmu dan memperindah wajahmu.'

16.iwh_en: It was narrated from Rafi' ibn 'Amr al-Muzani that he heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ say: 'Al-'Ajwa and the sacred rock are from Paradise' — or he said similar words.
16.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Rafi' ibn 'Amr al-Muzani bahwa ia mendengar Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Al-'Ajwa dan batu suci berasal dari surga' — atau beliau mengucapkan kata-kata yang serupa.'

17.iwh_en: It was narrated from Rafi' ibn 'Amr al-Muzani that he said: 'I heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ say: The Arabs will continue to have good as long as this practice of theirs is maintained among them. The Khawarij will shoot out of religion as an arrow shoots from its target, and they will not return to it. They are the worst of all creation.'
17.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Rafi' ibn 'Amr al-Muzani bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku mendengar Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: Orang-orang Arab akan terus memiliki kebaikan selama tradisi ini terpelihara di antara mereka. Kaum Khawarij keluar dari agama sebagaimana anak panah keluar dari sasarannya dan tidak akan kembali. Mereka adalah seburuk-buruk makhluk.'

18.iwh_en: It was narrated from Rafi' ibn 'Amr al-Muzani that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ mentioned a group and said: 'They shoot out of religion as an arrow shoots from its target, and they will not return to it. They are the worst of all creation and the worst of all beings.'
18.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Rafi' ibn 'Amr al-Muzani bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ menyebut suatu kaum dan bersabda: 'Mereka keluar dari agama sebagaimana anak panah keluar dari sasarannya dan tidak akan kembali. Mereka adalah seburuk-buruk makhluk dan ciptaan.'

19.iwh_en: It was narrated from a companion that at every gate of the city there was an angel with a drawn sword. He said: 'Then we continued until, when we reached the gate of the mosque, he said: When you see him, stand up.'
19.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari seorang sahabat bahwa di setiap pintu gerbang kota terdapat malaikat dengan pedang terhunus. Ia berkata: 'Kemudian kami terus berjalan hingga ketika kami sampai di pintu masjid, ia berkata: Bila engkau melihatnya, berdirilah.'

20.iwh_en: It was narrated from a companion that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ mentioned the Dajjal and said: 'He will come to a city, its inhabitants having left it, and it will be as if it is at its most prosperous. The Dajjal will come to it and find an angel at every gate with a drawn sword.'
20.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari seorang sahabat bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ menyebut Dajjal dan bersabda: 'Ia akan datang ke suatu kota yang telah ditinggalkan penduduknya, dan kota itu seolah-olah berada di puncak kemakmurannya. Dajjal akan mendatanginya dan mendapati malaikat di setiap pintu dengan pedang terhunus.'

21.iwh_en: It was narrated from a companion that they were with the Messenger of Allah ﷺ and a man stood up for so long in prayer that they almost felt sorry for him. When the Prophet ﷺ turned and finished, he said: 'O Messenger of Allah, that man stood up so long that we almost felt sorry for him.' He ﷺ said something in reply.
21.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari seorang sahabat bahwa mereka bersama Rasulullah ﷺ dan seseorang berdiri lama dalam shalat sehingga mereka hampir merasa kasihan padanya. Ketika Nabi ﷺ berpaling, ia berkata: 'Ya Rasulullah, orang itu berdiri sangat lama sehingga kami hampir merasa kasihan.' Beliau ﷺ menjawabnya.'

22.iwh_en: It was narrated from a companion that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ was asked about certain people and he said: 'These are those upon whom is anger' — pointing to the Jews — then he was asked about others and he said: 'These are those who have gone astray' — meaning the Christians.
22.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari seorang sahabat bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ ditanya tentang suatu kaum dan beliau bersabda: 'Mereka adalah orang-orang yang dimurkai' — sambil menunjuk ke arah orang-orang Yahudi — kemudian ditanya tentang kaum lain dan beliau bersabda: 'Mereka adalah orang-orang yang sesat' — yakni orang-orang Nasrani.'

23.iwh_en: It was narrated from Bahz that in his narration he said: 'The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: A tribulation will arise like the horns of cattle.' This is what Hisham said in the narration.
23.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Bahz bahwa dalam riwayatnya ia berkata: 'Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: Akan muncul fitnah seperti tanduk sapi.' Demikianlah yang Hisham sampaikan dalam riwayatnya.'

24.iwh_en: It was narrated from a companion that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'How will you be in a tribulation that erupts at the edges of the earth, like the horns of cattle?' They said: 'What should we do, O Prophet of Allah?' He said: 'Hold fast to this' — meaning the Quran and the Muslims.
24.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari seorang sahabat bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Bagaimana keadaan kalian dalam suatu fitnah yang memuncak di penjuru bumi seperti tanduk sapi?' Mereka berkata: 'Apa yang harus kami lakukan, wahai Nabi Allah?' Beliau bersabda: 'Berpeganglah pada ini' — yakni Al-Qur'an dan kaum muslimin.'

25.iwh_en: It was narrated from a companion that he saw at the top of the document Abu Bakr and 'Umar, and he thought that they would only be recorded there if it were for something good. So he said: 'Would you record my name, O Messenger of Allah?'
25.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari seorang sahabat bahwa ia melihat di bagian atas dokumen ada nama Abu Bakar dan Umar, dan ia mengira keduanya tidak akan dicatat di sana kecuali untuk kebaikan. Maka ia berkata: 'Apakah engkau akan mencatatkan namaku, wahai Rasulullah?'

26.iwh_en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn Hawalah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Whoever is saved from three things has indeed been saved.' He then mentioned them.
26.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari 'Abdullah ibn Hawalah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Barangsiapa selamat dari tiga hal, sungguh ia telah selamat.' Kemudian beliau menyebutkannya.'

27.iwh_en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn Hawalah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'You must go to al-Sham, you must go to al-Sham, you must go to al-Sham' — three times. 'And whoever refuses, let him go to Yemen.'
27.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari 'Abdullah ibn Hawalah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Pergilah ke Syam, pergilah ke Syam, pergilah ke Syam' — tiga kali. 'Dan barangsiapa menolak, pergilah ke Yaman.'

28.iwh_en: It was narrated from a companion that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, and Hisham narrated from him: 'I said, O Messenger of Allah, and they are saying that such and such did not happen.'
28.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari seorang sahabat bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda, dan Hisyam meriwayatkan darinya: 'Aku berkata: Ya Rasulullah, sementara mereka mengatakan bahwa hal itu tidak terjadi.'

29.iwh_en: It was narrated from a companion that a man said to him: 'Give up this she-camel for charity.' Then a man grabbed him and said: 'This man is giving this camel in charity, and by Allah it is better than him.' He replied: 'The Messenger of Allah ﷺ forbade killing a man's camel.'
29.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari seorang sahabat bahwa seseorang berkata kepadanya: 'Sedekahkanlah unta betina ini.' Kemudian seorang lelaki memegang lengannya dan berkata: 'Orang ini menyedekahkan untanya, demi Allah unta itu lebih baik darinya.' Ia menjawab: 'Rasulullah ﷺ melarang membunuh unta seseorang.'

30.iwh_en: It was narrated from Mu'awiya ibn Qurra, from his father (may Allah be pleased with him), that the Prophet ﷺ said something.
30.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Mu'awiyah ibn Qurra, dari ayahnya (radhiyallahu 'anhu), bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda sesuatu.'

31.iwh_en: It was narrated from Mu'awiya ibn Qurra, from his father (may Allah be pleased with him), that the Prophet ﷺ wiped over his head.
31.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Mu'awiyah ibn Qurra dari ayahnya (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ mengusap kepalanya.'

32.iwh_en: It was narrated from Mu'awiya ibn Qurra, from his father (may Allah be pleased with him), that a man said: 'O Messenger of Allah, I am slaughtering the sheep and I feel mercy for it.' The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'And if you show it mercy, Allah will show you mercy.'
32.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Mu'awiyah ibn Qurra dari ayahnya (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa seorang lelaki berkata: 'Ya Rasulullah, aku menyembelih kambing dan aku merasa kasihan padanya.' Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Jika engkau menyayanginya, Allah akan menyayangimu.'

33.iwh_en: It was narrated from Mu'awiya ibn Qurra, from his father (may Allah be pleased with him), that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Fasting three days of each month is like fasting for a lifetime.'
33.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Mu'awiyah ibn Qurra dari ayahnya (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Berpuasa tiga hari setiap bulan adalah seperti berpuasa sepanjang masa.'

34.iwh_en: It was narrated from Mu'awiya ibn Qurra, from his father (may Allah be pleased with him), that the Prophet ﷺ said to his father: 'Do you not love that you will not come to any gate of the gates of Paradise except that you will enter through it?'
34.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Mu'awiyah ibn Qurra dari ayahnya (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ berkata kepada ayahnya: 'Apakah engkau tidak suka bahwa engkau tidak akan mendatangi satu pun pintu surga kecuali engkau masuk melaluinya?'

35.iwh_en: It was narrated from Mu'awiya ibn Qurra, from his father (may Allah be pleased with him), that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'When the people of al-Sham become corrupt, there is no good remaining in you.'
35.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Mu'awiyah ibn Qurra dari ayahnya (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Apabila penduduk Syam telah rusak, maka tidak ada kebaikan yang tersisa pada kalian.'

36.iwh_en: It was narrated from a companion from Muzayna that he said: 'We came to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ in a group from Muzayna and pledged allegiance to him. His shirt was open at the collar. I sought to take hold of his hand and entered my hand into his collar to touch the seal of prophethood.'
36.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari seorang sahabat dari Muzaynah bahwa ia berkata: 'Kami datang kepada Rasulullah ﷺ dalam sekelompok orang dari Muzaynah dan membai'at beliau. Bajunya terbuka di kerahnya. Aku berusaha memegang tangannya dan memasukkan tanganku ke dalam kerahnya untuk menyentuh cap kenabian.'

37.iwh_en: It was narrated from a companion that he sought permission from the Messenger of Allah ﷺ to put his hand into his collar so that he might pray for him. Nothing stopped the Prophet ﷺ from praying for him, and while he was touching the seal, the Prophet ﷺ prayed for him.
37.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari seorang sahabat bahwa ia meminta izin dari Rasulullah ﷺ untuk memasukkan tangannya ke dalam kerah beliau agar beliau bisa mendoakannya. Tidak ada yang menghalangi Nabi ﷺ untuk mendoakannya, dan sementara ia menyentuh cap kenabian, Nabi ﷺ mendoakannya.'

38.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Iyas, from his father, that he came to the Prophet ﷺ and pledged allegiance to him, and the Prophet ﷺ prayed for him.
38.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Iyas dari ayahnya bahwa ia datang kepada Nabi ﷺ dan membai'at beliau, dan Nabi ﷺ mendoakannya.'

39.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Iyas, from his father (may Allah be pleased with him), that the Prophet ﷺ said regarding fasting three days of each month: 'Fasting the whole year is like that.'
39.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Iyas dari ayahnya (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda tentang puasa tiga hari setiap bulan: 'Itu seperti berpuasa sepanjang tahun.'

40.iwh_en: It was narrated from a companion that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said regarding a tribulation that would spread across the ends of the earth like the horns of cattle: 'What should we do, O Prophet of Allah?' He said: 'Hold fast to this' — meaning the Quran and his companions.
40.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari seorang sahabat bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda tentang fitnah yang akan menyebar ke penjuru bumi seperti tanduk sapi: 'Apa yang harus kami lakukan, wahai Nabi Allah?' Beliau bersabda: 'Berpeganglah pada ini' — yakni Al-Qur'an dan para sahabatnya.'

41.iwh_en: It was narrated from a companion that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Indeed they are being punished, and they are not being punished for something great' — yet it is great. He ﷺ then said: 'Which of you will bring me a fresh palm branch?' He broke it in two and placed one piece on each grave.
41.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari seorang sahabat bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Sesungguhnya keduanya sedang diazab, dan keduanya tidak diazab karena sesuatu yang besar' — padahal itu besar. Kemudian beliau bersabda: 'Siapa di antara kalian yang membawakan aku pelepah kurma yang segar?' Beliau memecahnya menjadi dua dan meletakkan satu bagian di setiap kuburan.'

42.iwh_en: It was narrated from a companion that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Among the sins that are hastened in punishment in this world along with what is stored for the next life are transgression and severing ties of kinship.'
42.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari seorang sahabat bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Di antara dosa-dosa yang disegerakan hukumannya di dunia bersamaan dengan apa yang ditangguhkan di akhirat adalah kezaliman dan memutus silaturahmi.'

43.iwh_en: It was narrated from a companion that he said: 'We were with the Messenger of Allah ﷺ and we were almost trotting along with him.' Waki' said: 'Almost running.'
43.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari seorang sahabat bahwa ia berkata: 'Kami bersama Rasulullah ﷺ dan kami hampir berlari-lari kecil bersamanya.' Waki' berkata: 'Hampir berlari.'

44.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'I heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ say: Seek it in the last ten nights — meaning Laylat al-Qadr — in the odd-numbered nights.'
44.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku mendengar Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: Carilah ia di sepuluh malam terakhir — yakni Lailatul Qadar — pada malam-malam ganjil.'

45.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Whoever kills a person under a treaty unjustly, Allah will forbid him Paradise.'
45.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Barangsiapa membunuh seorang muahad (orang yang mendapat perlindungan perjanjian) secara tidak benar, maka Allah mengharamkan surga baginya.'

46.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn Abi Bakra, from his father, that the Prophet ﷺ said: 'A man from among my nation will come forth and will mediate between the people' — or similar words.
46.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibn Abi Bakra dari ayahnya bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Seorang lelaki dari umatku akan muncul dan akan mendamaikan di antara manusia' — atau kata-kata yang serupa.'

47.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn Abi Bakra, from his father (may Allah be pleased with him), that he wrote that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'A judge must not judge between two people while he is angry.'
47.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibn Abi Bakra dari ayahnya (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia menulis bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Seorang hakim tidak boleh memutuskan perkara antara dua orang ketika ia sedang marah.'

48.iwh_en: It was narrated from a freed slave of Abu Bakra, from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him), that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Two sins — there is no sin Allah hastens punishment for more than transgression and severing ties of kinship.'
48.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari seorang bekas budak Abu Bakra dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Dua dosa — tidak ada dosa yang Allah percepat hukumannya lebih dari kezaliman dan memutus silaturahmi.'

49.iwh_en: It was narrated from Muslim ibn Abi Bakra, from his father Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him), that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'People of certain characteristics will emerge from the east shooting arrows.'
49.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Muslim ibn Abi Bakra dari ayahnya Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Orang-orang dengan ciri-ciri tertentu akan muncul dari timur melemparkan anak panah.'

50.iwh_en: It was narrated from Muslim ibn Abi Bakra, from his father Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him), that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Soon a group will emerge from the east who recite the Quran but it does not pass their throats.'
50.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Muslim ibn Abi Bakra dari ayahnya Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Segera akan muncul suatu kaum dari timur yang membaca Al-Qur'an namun tidak melewati tenggorokan mereka.'
